setting.note: A six-generation family saga anchored in Tbilisi, where a chocolate-maker's secret recipe carries a curse down the century. The map holds the old town below Narikala, the grand houses of Sololaki at the foot of Mtatsminda, and the parade of Rustaveli Avenue through the Soviet years. The story ranges far, to Petrograd, Moscow, London and Berlin, but its heart stays in the Georgian capital.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Nino Haratischwili, first published in German as Das achte Leben (Für Brilka) in 2014. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Eighth Life, Nino Haratischwili (2014). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The deep red-brown of the family's hot chocolate, the sweet inheritance that runs through the saga. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
